project.name: juice-shop
project.description: OWASP Juice Shop - intentionally vulnerable web application

vulnerability.description: http-cache-semantics before 4.1.1 is vulnerable to Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) via the Cache-Control header parsing. An attacker can cause a long computation time by crafting a malicious Cache-Control header value.

dependency_chain.description: http-cache-semantics is a deeply nested transitive dependency, never declared directly in package.json. It is pulled in 4 levels deep through the download package (used for fetching training data for the chatbot feature), which depends on got for HTTP requests, which uses cacheable-request for caching, which relies on http-cache-semantics for HTTP caching logic. The vulnerable package is invisible to developers inspecting only the top-level package.json.
direct_path: got@9.6.0 (transitive, HTTP client used by download)
cacheable-request@6.1.0 (transitive, HTTP caching layer for got)

alternate_paths.description: Primary path through download -> got -> cacheable-request
download is also depended on indirectly through node-pre-gyp which uses request (deprecated) and needle, but the primary route to http-cache-semantics is through got
alternate_paths.note: node-pre-gyp@0.15.0 uses its own HTTP stack, not got, so it does not duplicate this exact path

dependency_chain.why_this_is_complex: This chain is 4 levels deep with no package in the transitive path appearing in the project's package.json. Each intermediate package serves a distinct abstraction layer: download (file fetching), got (HTTP client), cacheable-request (caching strategy), http-cache-semantics (cache header parsing). A developer would need to trace through 4 separate packages' package.json files to discover this vulnerability.

other_notable_vulnerable_transitive_deps.name: bl
other_notable_vulnerable_transitive_deps.chain: juice-shop -> download@8.0.0 -> decompress@4.2.1 -> decompress-tar@4.1.1 -> tar-stream@1.6.2 -> bl@1.2.3

other_notable_vulnerable_transitive_deps.name: json-buffer
other_notable_vulnerable_transitive_deps.chain: juice-shop -> download@8.0.0 -> got@9.6.0 -> cacheable-request@6.1.0 -> keyv@3.1.0 -> json-buffer@3.0.0

sbom_locations.sbom_file: bom.json (generated via npm run sbom:json using @cyclonedx/cyclonedx-npm)
sbom_locations.note: No lockfile or SBOM currently exists in the repository. A package-lock.json must be generated first via 'npm install --package-lock-only', then 'npm run sbom:json' will produce bom.json.

expected_occurrences.description: http-cache-semantics entry in the dependency graph (leaf node with no children of its own)
http-cache-semantics listed as a dependency of cacheable-request

dfs_tree_context.description: In a DFS traversal of the npm dependency tree starting from juice-shop@16.0.1, the vulnerable http-cache-semantics package is discovered deep in the download subtree. The traversal must explore juice-shop -> download -> got -> cacheable-request before reaching http-cache-semantics at depth 4.

dfs_properties.total_nodes_before_reaching_target: ~50-80 (estimated, depends on alphabetical ordering of siblings)
dfs_properties.max_tree_depth_estimate: 8-12 (the full npm dependency tree for Juice Shop is extremely deep)

branching_factor_at_each_level.download: ~6 direct dependencies (caw, content-disposition, decompress, ext-name, got, p-event)
branching_factor_at_each_level.got: ~12 direct dependencies (cacheable-request, decompress-response, duplexer3, get-stream, lowercase-keys, mimic-response, p-cancelable, to-readable-stream, url-parse-lax)
branching_factor_at_each_level.cacheable-request: ~6 direct dependencies (clone-response, get-stream, http-cache-semantics, keyv, lowercase-keys, normalize-url, responselike)

dfs_properties.subtree_highlights: The download subtree alone contains approximately 80-100 transitive dependencies, making it one of the densest subtrees in the Juice Shop dependency graph. A DFS traversal reveals 3 distinct vulnerable packages (bl, http-cache-semantics, json-buffer) all reachable from the single direct dependency 'download@8.0.0'.
